body.raw:
# Tooltip

## Notice

By default, when the media size is less than 1200px, the `Tooltip` will automatically place to a fixed position (`bottom` by default) instead of following the cursor. We will treat media size greater than or equal to 1200px (defined by `followMediaSize`) as PC devices, and others is mobile or tablet devices. If you want to change this default behavior, you can set `followMediaSize` to `0` or other values you want.

## Import

```jsx live=false
import { Tooltip } from '@node-real/bee';
```

## Usage

If the children of `Tooltip` is a `string`, we should wrap with in a `span`. If the `Tooltip` is wrapping a functional component, ensure that the functional component accepts a `ref` using `forwardRef`.

```jsx
<Tooltip
  content={
    'If the children of Tooltip is a string, we should wrap with in a span. If the Tooltip is wrapping a functional component, ensure that the functional component accepts a ref using forwardRef.'
  }
>
  <Button>Show Tooltip</Button>
</Tooltip>
```

## Placement

To take it effect, you need to set isFollowCursor to false.

```jsx
function Example() {
  const placements = [
    'auto',
    'auto-start',
    'auto-end',
    'top',
    'top-start',
    'top-end',
    'bottom',
    'bottom-start',
    'bottom-end',
    'right',
    'right-start',
    'right-end',
    'left',
    'left-start',
    'left-end',
  ];

  return (
    <SimpleGrid columns={3} gap={24}>
      {placements.map((item, index) => (
        <Tooltip content={item} isFollowCursor={false} placement={item} key={item}>
          <Button size="rg">{item}</Button>
        </Tooltip>
      ))}
    </SimpleGrid>
  );
}
```

## Disabled

```jsx
<Wrap>
  <Tooltip content={'Some text in tooltip.'} isDisabled>
    <Button isDisabled={true}>Show Tooltip</Button>
  </Tooltip>

  <Tooltip content={'Some text in tooltip.'} isDisabled>
    <Button>Show Tooltip</Button>
  </Tooltip>
</Wrap>
```

##

    },

      default: '',
      desc: 'The placement of tooltip, to take it effect, you need to set isFollowCursor to false.',
    },
    {
      name: 'onHide',
      required: false,
      type: '() => void',
      default: '',
      desc: 'Invoked once when tooltip begins to hide.',
    },
  ]}
/>
